texto: We have trauma, and we have grief. People die, and we find it baffling. Painful. Inexplicable. Grief is baffling. There are theories on how we react to loss and death, how we cope, how we handle loss. Some believe the range of emotions mourners experience is predictable, that grief can be monitored, as if mourners are following a checklist. But sorrow is less of a checklist, more like water. It's fluid, it has no set shape, never disappears, never ends. It doesn't go away. It just changes. It changes us.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mira Ptacin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “We have trauma, and we have grief. People die, and we find it baffling. Painful. Inexplicable. Gr…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, death, grief pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e mudanca.
